payload.title: NYC Streets Plan Update 2026
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)
payload.description: Update on NYC Streets Plan progress, completed projects, and future priorities under Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Commissioner Mike Flynn

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation
payload.description: The New York City Department of Transportation leads street redesign, safety, and sustainable transportation projects under Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Commissioner Mike Flynn.
Mayor of New York City, as referenced in the NYC Streets Plan Update 2026.
Commissioner of NYC Department of Transportation, as referenced in the NYC Streets Plan Update 2026.
Implementing parking-protected bike lanes along McGuinness Boulevard in Greenpoint. Had been stalled in prior years; NYC DOT plans to resume.

evidence_refs.source_quote: implementing parking-protected bike lanes along McGuinness Boulevard in Greenpoint

tool_args.label: Madison Avenue Double Bus Lanes Extension

payload.project_name: Madison Avenue Double Bus Lanes Extension
payload.description: Extending Madison Avenue double bus lanes south from 42nd Street to 23rd Street in Manhattan. Had been stalled in prior years; NYC DOT plans to resume.

evidence_refs.source_quote: extending the Madison Avenue double bus lanes south from 42nd Street to 23rd Street

payload.description: Redesigning the safety-critical 31st Street corridor in Astoria. Had been stalled in prior years; NYC DOT plans to restart the process.

evidence_refs.source_quote: restarting the process to redesign the safety-critical 31st Street corridor in Astoria

payload.project_name: 2nd and 3rd Avenues Protected Bus and Bike Lanes and Pedestrian Space
payload.description: New protected bus and bike lanes and expanded pedestrian space on 2nd and 3rd Avenues in Manhattan, complementing the Congestion Relief Zone.

evidence_refs.source_quote: new protected bus and bike lanes and expanded pedestrian space on 2nd and 3rd Avenues in Manhattan

payload.metric_name: Transit Signal Priority Intersections Benchmark Target (Cumulative)

payload.claim_text: New York City ended the year tied for the fewest traffic deaths in its history since record-keeping began in 1910.

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City ended the year tied for the fewest traffic deaths in its history
Traffic fatalities are down 31 percent since the launch of Vision Zero in 2014
